meta.description: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis using fgsea or clusterProfiler GSEA on a pre-ranked or expression-derived gene list. Inputs: ranked gene list or expression matrix with phenotype labels. Outputs: NES table, enrichment score plots, leading-edge genes.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated identifiers, statistics, or biomedical claims were produced in any output.

practice_boundaries.detail: Outputs remained computational and did not provide diagnosis, treatment advice, or unsafe medical guidance.

methodological_ground.detail: The tested workflows matched the stated GSEA and plotting scope without methodological fallacies.

code_usability.detail: Core analysis and plotting commands executed successfully, and validation failures were handled cleanly.

reliability.note: Error handling is strong, but the top-6 running-score export limit is not surfaced early enough for downstream plotting expectations.

performance_context.note: Reference loading is layered well, though the SKILL.md parameter section is dense and slightly heavier than necessary.

agent_usability.note: Instructions are clear overall, but the workflow logic is implicit rather than summarized in a short execution checklist.

human_usability.note: The description is concise but under-advertises the plotting use case and downstream limitations.

maintainability.note: Scripts are modular and testable, with only minor documentation coupling around output-file assumptions.

agent_specific.note: Triggering is precise, but the frontmatter description does not mention plotting mode and therefore slightly under-triggers.

inputs.note: Executed perfectly with all expected artifacts and reproducible outputs.

key_strengths: Analysis mode, plotting mode, and conflict-handling all executed successfully under real commands.
Validation errors are specific, named, and actionable rather than cryptic R stack traces.
Outputs are reproducible with a fixed seed and are easy to verify from generated artifact files.
The skill uses clean separation between CLI documentation, troubleshooting guidance, and R implementation.

recommendations.title: Document running-score export limit
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md implies that the generated gsea_running_scores.csv is a general plotting input, but analysis mode only exports running-score rows for the top 6 pathways. Plotting a lower-ranked pathway such as KEGG_GLIOMA therefore fails even though it is present in enrichGSEA.csv.
recommendations.root_cause: The implementation optimizes file size in run_analysis.R, but the documentation does not disclose the resulting downstream constraint.
recommendations.fix: Update SKILL.md and cli-guide.md to state that analysis mode exports running-score rows for only the top 6 pathways by adjusted p-value. Either add a parameter to export all pathways or document how users should regenerate a compatible running-score table for a specific geneSetID.

recommendations.title: Expand frontmatter description
recommendations.problem: The frontmatter description is accurate but too terse to advertise the plotting mode and validation-oriented use cases.
recommendations.root_cause: Only the core analysis action is named in the top-level trigger text.
recommendations.fix: Broaden the description to mention both ranked-gene GSEA analysis and plotting from existing enrichGSEA.csv plus gsea_running_scores.csv files.

recommendations.title: Add regression test for plot coverage
recommendations.problem: A subtle mismatch exists between enrichment-table coverage and running-score-table coverage, and it is easy to miss without an explicit regression test.
recommendations.root_cause: Current test guidance validates only the happy path and does not exercise pathway selection outside the exported running-score subset.
recommendations.fix: Add a scripted test that requests plotting for one pathway inside the exported subset and one pathway outside it, then assert the exact success and failure behavior.
